us-politics.headline: Trump Reshapes GOP, Sparking Internal Party Tensions
us-politics.body:
Former President Donald Trump continues to exert significant influence over the Republican Party, triggering internal conflicts. GOP senators warn that Trump's retribution tour could undermine the party's legislative agenda, with some endorsements creating friction among established members.

Trump's primary push has already reshaped congressional dynamics. Some Republicans fear his interventions might create short-term challenges for party unity and electoral strategy, particularly in swing districts where moderate voters remain uncertain about supporting Democratic alternatives.

sources.title: GOP senators warn Trump retribution tour hurts president’s agenda
Trump’s primary push could leave him with short-term problem in Congress
Even as anxieties grow under Trump, these swing voters aren't ready to back Democrats
Trump owns the GOP. Could Republicans pay the price in the midterms?

us-politics.headline: Immigration and Border Policy Spark Heated Political Debate
us-politics.body:
A new executive order targeting Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has ignited controversy, with legal experts calling it political theater. The order, proposed by Representative Abigail Spanberger, highlights the ongoing tensions surrounding immigration policy.

The debate reflects deeper divisions about border security, immigration enforcement, and the balance between national security and humanitarian considerations. Different political perspectives continue to clash over the most effective approach to managing immigration challenges.

sources.title: Legal expert unleashes on Spanberger's new executive order targeting ICE: 'Political theater'
Castro charges fuel debate over military action in Cuba
Liberal U.S. mayors team up with European counterparts to fight authoritarianism

au-politics.headline: Budget Tensions Rise as Unemployment Jumps to 4.5%
au-politics.body:
The federal government faces mounting pressure as Australia's unemployment rate climbed to 4.5%, challenging the Albanese administration's economic narrative. Markets have sharply reduced expectations of further interest rate hikes, signaling economic uncertainty.

Opposition leaders are positioning themselves to critique the budget, with Pauline Hanson proposing alternative gas policies that challenge current taxation approaches. The economic landscape appears increasingly complex, with political parties seeking to differentiate their economic strategies.

sources.title: Albanese's hard sell as budget opponents line up
Australia’s unemployment rate jumps to 4.5% with markets slashing chance of more interest rate hikes
Pauline Hanson announces Norway-inspired gas policy as she decries 25% export tax as ‘economic vandalism’
Hanson proposes tax break for drilling alongside new gas royalty

au-politics.headline: High Court to Hear Neo-Nazi Group's Political Party Bid
au-politics.body:
A controversial neo-Nazi group has secured a High Court hearing in its attempt to be registered as a political party - a move that has sparked significant debate about free speech and hate speech limits. The case represents a critical test of Australia's political registration laws.

The hearing will examine the group's right to formal political recognition, with potential implications for how Australia defines acceptable political discourse. Civil liberties and anti-racism advocates are watching the proceedings closely.

sources.title: Neo-Nazi group secures high court hearing over hate listing as it seeks to become a political party

world.headline: Iran-US War Enters Critical Phase as Mediation Efforts Intensify
world.body:
Diplomatic tensions are reaching a boiling point in the ongoing conflict between Iran and the United States. Pakistan has stepped up mediation efforts as Tehran reviews a new US proposal to end the war. The conflict - now in its 83rd day - continues to send shockwaves through global geopolitics.

Economic analysts warn of four distinct waves of global economic impact from the prolonged conflict. Meanwhile, international observers are closely watching potential diplomatic breakthroughs that could de-escalate the increasingly complex regional standoff.

sources.title: What options do the US and Iran have left to bring war to an end?
Iran reviews US proposal to end war as Pakistan steps up mediation efforts
The economic shock of the Iran war will hit the world in four waves
Iran war day 83: Tehran ‘reviewing’ latest US response to end conflict

world.headline: Ebola Outbreak Disrupts DR Congo World Cup Preparations
world.body:
A new Ebola outbreak has forced the Democratic Republic of Congo to cancel its World Cup training camp, highlighting the ongoing public health challenges facing the nation. Health authorities are scrambling to contain the spread of a potentially dangerous new strain of the virus.

The cancellation underscores the persistent threat of infectious diseases in the region. Scientists are racing to develop a vaccine that could provide protection against this latest outbreak, which threatens both sporting preparations and public health.

sources.title: DR Congo cancels World Cup training camp over Ebola outbreak
Ebola outbreak: When will a vaccine be developed for the new strain?
DR Congo cancel World Cup training camp in Kinshasa over Ebola outbreak

world.headline: Global Tech and Economic Tensions Simmer in Shifting Landscape
world.body:
Nvidia's latest financial results failed to impress investors, signaling potential cooling in the tech sector. The company's performance reflects broader economic uncertainties and shifting market dynamics.

In another economic indicator, South Korea's stock market saw a boost after Samsung's union called off a planned strike. These developments point to ongoing volatility in global markets and the delicate balance of corporate and labor interests.

sources.title: Nvidia's latest record result fails to impress investors
South Korea’s stock market soars as Samsung union calls off planned strike

ai.headline: SpaceX IPO Reveals Massive AI Spending and Risks
ai.body: SpaceX is preparing to go public, and its IPO filing exposes the aggressive financial strategy behind Elon Musk's AI ventures. The company burned $6.4 billion last year on xAI, with ongoing massive investments in data center infrastructure - including a $2.8 billion purchase of gas turbines. Notably, the filing lists Grok's 'spicy' mode as a potential risk factor, highlighting the unconventional approach of Musk's AI projects.

sources.title: In SpaceX’s IPO, Elon Musk is a risk factor
SpaceX Listed Grok’s ‘Spicy’ Mode as a Risk in Its IPO Filing
SpaceX Is Spending $2.8 Billion to Buy Gas Turbines for Its AI Data Centers
Famously secret about its finances, SpaceX opens its books for the first time
xAI burned $6.4B last year — SpaceX’s IPO filing shows why the spending is far from over
Musk’s xAI is being sued over its data center generators — now it’s buying $2.8B more

ai.headline: Tech Giants Shuffle Resources as AI Race Intensifies
ai.body: Meta is cutting thousands of jobs to offset massive AI investments, signaling the high-stakes financial reallocation happening across the tech industry. Meanwhile, Nvidia continues its remarkable growth - posting another record quarter and revealing $43 billion in startup holdings. Anthropic is showing signs of financial maturity, projecting its first profitable quarter and securing substantial compute resources from xAI at $1.25 billion per month.

sources.title: Meta lays off thousands of employees to offset AI investments
Jensen Huang says he’s found a ‘brand new’ $200B market for Nvidia
Anthropic says it’s about to have its first profitable quarter
Nvidia posts another record quarter, reveals $43B of holdings in startups
Anthropic will pay xAI $1.25B per month for compute

btc.headline: Bitcoin Options Market Heats Up Before Major Expiry
btc.body: Traders are flooding the Bitcoin options market ahead of a massive $6 billion expiration on May 29. Significant activity centers around $82,000 call options, creating tension around potential price movements. The market sits in a delicate balance - caught between the max pain point at $75,000 and a substantial call option wall at $80,000.

sources.title: The $6 billion expiration countdown: Traders pile into $82,000 bitcoin calls ahead of May 29 expiry

btc.headline: SpaceX Reveals Substantial Bitcoin Treasury in IPO Filing
btc.body: Elon Musk's SpaceX has disclosed larger-than-expected Bitcoin holdings in its initial public offering documents. The revelation signals continued corporate confidence in cryptocurrency despite market volatility. This move follows Musk's previous cryptocurrency investments and could further legitimize Bitcoin as a corporate treasury asset.

sources.title: SpaceX reveals larger-than-expected Bitcoin holdings in IPO filing

btc.headline: Nakamoto Bitcoin Firm Struggles with Dramatic Stock Collapse
btc.body: Bitcoin technology firm Nakamoto is pursuing a dramatic 1-for-40 reverse stock split after experiencing a catastrophic 99% price plunge. The move aims to stabilize its Nasdaq-listed shares and prevent potential delisting. Investors face significant dilution as the company attempts to reset its market valuation.

sources.title: Bitcoin treasury Nakamoto plans reverse stock split to save ailing share price
Bitcoin Firm Nakamoto Plots 1-for-40 Stock Split Following 99% Price Plunge

food.name: Hokkaido Milk Bread Rolls
food.description: Ultra-soft, cloud-like bread rolls with a delicate, pillowy texture that melts in your mouth
food.tip: Use tangzhong method (pre-cooking a small portion of flour and liquid) to ensure maximum softness and moisture

health.name: Desk Spine Decompression
health.description: A targeted micro-movement sequence to reset spinal alignment and release tension accumulated from prolonged sitting
health.tip: Use a doorframe as a guide: stand with your back against it, slowly slide down 2-3 inches, hold for 30 seconds, then rise

learn.description: The emerging field of designing cities and infrastructure by directly copying strategies found in natural ecosystems
learn.why: It offers revolutionary approaches to sustainable urban development by learning from billions of years of nature's problem-solving
